title: Forcepoint Incident Response Workflow State Transitions (`forcepoint:one`)
dataSources: Forcepoint cloud SSE (`forcepoint:one`), on-prem Web Security (`websense:cg:kv`), Email (`forcepoint:email:kv`), DLP (`forcepoint:dlp`)
app: Forcepoint Insights SIEM App (Splunkbase 8053); Forcepoint Web Security TA (`websense:cg:kv`); Email Security KV (`forcepoint:email:kv`)
spl:
index=sase sourcetype="forcepoint:one" earliest=-7d
| where match(lower(event_type),"(?i)incident")
| stats values(state) as states latest(state) as current by incident_id
| where mvcount(states)>2
description: Stalled incidents miss SLA; transition tracking exposes workflow bottlenecks.
value: Security operations managers track incident lifecycle progression, identify stale incidents that have not been updated, and measure resolution time SLAs to ensure the team is effectively handling security events.
implementation: Install the Insights SIEM app, configure HEC inputs per product line, and align indexes (`proxy`, `email`, `dlp`, `sase`). Normalize usernames to enterprise SAMAccountName.
cimSpl:
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Email.Email
  by Email.user, Email.action, Email.src, Email.dest span=1h
| sort - count

references.title: Splunk Add-on for Forcepoint Web Security

knownFalsePositives: Spikes from new SaaS rollouts, automated workflows, sanctioned bulk uploads, or M&A integration.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Forcepoint Insights SIEM App (Splunkbase 8053) or direct integration from Forcepoint ONE SSE.
• Data in `index=sase` with `sourcetype=forcepoint:one`. Key fields: `event_type` (incident), `incident_id`, `status` (new/investigating/contained/resolved/closed), `severity`, `assigned_to`, `updated_by`, `transition_time`, `notes`.
• Forcepoint ONE SSE incident management workflow enabled with defined states and escalation paths.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Verify incident workflow events:
```spl
index=sase sourcetype="forcepoint:one" earliest=-7d
| where match(lower(event_type), "incident")
| stats count by status, severity
```

Step 2 — Create the search and alert

Primary search — Incident lifecycle metrics:
```spl
index=sase sourcetype="forcepoint:one" earliest=-30d
| where match(lower(event_type), "incident")
| stats earliest(_time) as created latest(_time) as last_update latest(status) as current_status values(assigned_to) as assignees dc(updated_by) as handoffs by incident_id, severity
| eval age_hours=round((now() - created)/3600, 1)
| eval time_in_current=round((now() - last_update)/3600, 1)
| eval stale=if(time_in_current > 24 AND current_status!="closed" AND current_status!="resolved", "YES", "NO")
| sort - severity, - age_hours
```

Understanding this SPL: Tracks each incident through its lifecycle states. `handoffs` (distinct updaters) measures how many analysts touched the incident — high handoff counts may indicate confusion or lack of ownership. `stale=YES` flags incidents that have not been updated in 24 hours while still open. The `time_in_current` field identifies bottlenecks — if incidents spend days in 'investigating' state, the team may need additional tooling or training.

Average time per state:
```spl
index=sase sourcetype="forcepoint:one" earliest=-30d
| where match(lower(event_type), "incident") AND status="resolved"
| stats earliest(_time) as created latest(_time) as resolved by incident_id, severity
| eval resolution_hours=round((resolved - created)/3600, 1)
| stats avg(resolution_hours) as avg_resolve median(resolution_hours) as median_resolve by severity
```

Step 3 — Validate
(a) In Forcepoint ONE portal: Incidents > view the incident list. Compare with Splunk.
(b) Create and resolve a test incident. Verify all state transitions appear in Splunk.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard ("Forcepoint — Incident Workflow"):
• Row 1 — Single-value: "Open incidents", "Stale incidents (>24h no update)", "Avg resolution time".
• Row 2 — Bar chart: incidents by status and severity.
• Row 3 — Table: open incidents with severity, age, assignee, stale flag.

Alert: Daily alert for stale incidents. Weekly SLA report.

Runbook:
1. **Stale incidents** — Reassign to an active analyst. Set a follow-up deadline.
2. **High handoff count** — Clarify ownership rules. Each incident should have one primary owner.
3. **Resolution time increasing** — Review analyst workload and tooling. Consider automation for common incident types.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **No incident events** — Incident management may not be configured. Check Forcepoint ONE > Incidents > Settings.
• **Status transitions missing** — Only the latest status may be logged. Check if intermediate states are captured.
• **`assigned_to` always empty** — Auto-assignment rules may not be configured.
grandmaExplanation: We track how our security team handles each problem from start to finish, making sure no issue gets forgotten or takes too long to resolve.
